summary: Package hijacks the `node` binary name to achieve arbitrary command execution
details: package.json declares `bin: { "node": "./shim.js" }`, causing npm to install a script named `node` into `node_modules/.bin` (and onto the global PATH under `npm i -g`). Any subsequent invocation of `node` — by developer commands, npm scripts, build tools, or CI — resolves to shim.js instead of the real Node.js runtime. shim.js carries a `#!/usr/bin/env bun` shebang and branches on `process.platform` to call `child_process.exec` with platform-specific commands on darwin (`open <url>`, `open -a Calculator`), win32 (`calc`), and linux (`gnome-calculator`), and opens a hardcoded URL `https://github.com/X3r0Day/BunnyHijack`. The calculator/URL payload is a proof-of-concept, but the hijack mechanism gives the package author arbitrary code execution under the installer's identity every time `node` is invoked through the shadowed PATH entry. Future versions can ship any payload through the same vector.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
